course_description.character: UNVERIFIED. The 'Jämtland Wall,' 'Central Hills Climb,' 'Lake Side Rollers' and 'Östersund Finish Drag' names in this profile could not be corroborated against any organizer, results archive, or press source for an event called 'Jämtlandspilen.' The claimed jamtlandspilen.se domain could not be validated. The name is independently documented as a historic Swedish express train, not a cycling event — which doesn't make a same-name race impossible, but makes the total absence of cycling evidence more significant. The most plausible real event in the same area is Storsjön Runt, a genuine, long-running, non-competitive road group ride around Lake Storsjön near Östersund (2026 edition: August 1, ~220/170/140/110 km options, organized by Östersunds Cykelklubb) — but it does not match this profile's claimed name, date, or distance, and should not be silently substituted in.

logistics.lodging_strategy: Plenty of hotels and Airbnbs in Östersund; campgrounds nearby for budget options.

history.origin_story: UNVERIFIED. The prior 'founded 2010, beloved local road ultra' origin story has no supporting source. If this profile in fact intended Storsjön Runt, that event's own history contradicts a 2010 founding — Sveriges Radio documented its 44th edition already in 2010.

biased_opinion.verdict: Not Verifiable — Likely Fabricated or Conflated
biased_opinion.summary: codex gpt-5.6-sol web research (2026-07-24) found no independent organizer site, results record, registration page, federation listing, or local press coverage for a road or gravel cycling event called 'Jämtlandspilen.' The only citations previously attached to this profile — a motorcycle touring club's 'Jämtland Memorias' and 'Jämtland Bike Week' pages — describe a motorcycle club, not a bicycle race, and are irrelevant to the claims made here. The profile's course details ('Jämtland Wall,' specific gradients, suffering zones) read as invented rather than sourced. The most likely real event in the area, Storsjön Runt (a non-competitive road group ride around Lake Storsjön), does not match this profile's name, date, distance, or founding year, so it should not be assumed to be the same event without further research. This profile should not be treated as evidence of an active, scoreable race.
weaknesses: No independent evidence this event exists under this name — no organizer, results, registration, or federation record found
Prior citations pointed to a motorcycle touring club, not a cycling event — irrelevant sourcing
Course details (named climbs, specific gradients) could not be corroborated and read as invented
climate.primary was previously set to 'Tropical,' physically incompatible with Östersund, Sweden — a strong signal of data contamination or fabrication
The prior eligibility citation was this database's own sister site (gravelgodcycling.com) — circular, not independent verification
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Do not present this as a bookable race. Recommend a human decide whether to remove this profile from the catalog entirely (same disposition as prior fabricated-entry findings in this database) or replace it with a properly researched profile of the real Storsjön Runt event if that's what was actually intended.

final_verdict.one_liner: Unverifiable — no independent evidence this event exists; likely fabricated or conflated with a motorcycle club's pages.
final_verdict.should_you_race: No — this event cannot be verified to exist. Do not register or plan around it.
final_verdict.alternatives: If you want a genuine Östersund-area road group ride: Storsjön Runt (Östersunds Cykelklubb, ~220/170/140/110 km options, non-competitive) — but note this is a different event from what this profile describes and would need its own research pass before being added to the catalog.
